name: Beans Bourgignon
recipeIngredient: 1 large carrot, cut into half rounds
1 tsp dried thyme (use at least 1 tbsp if fresh)
1 1/2 cups hearty red wine (more if using to saute)
(Do not skimp on quality; use a wine you would drink.)
2 + cups water or hearty vegetable broth (flavor with dried vegetable broth and in the days prior to McD, with Bovril or another meat broth)
recipeInstructions:
1. Begin by sautéing onions until translucent.
2. Add potatoes and carrots. Sauté briefly before adding water or broth, tomato paste, thyme, and bay leaves.
3. Bring to a boil, then simmer until the potatoes and carrots are cooked (about 20 minutes), adding extra liquid as needed to keep the vegetables covered.
4. Toward the end of cooking, add wine, beans, garlic, and salt to taste. Bring to a boil again and simmer for an additional 5 minutes.
5. Sauté mushrooms in your choice of liquid, combine with the bean mixture, and serve.
6. This dish pairs well with crusty whole wheat bread and a salad. It can also be served over brown rice or as a topping for baked potatoes. The flavor improves if left overnight to sit.
